conflict: The strangeness of the manuscript itself — it resists comprehension, its materials are alien, its notation moves in ways that are not wizarding-photograph movement but something representational and unfamiliar
consequence: Gavin has the manuscript but cannot read it. Obsession begins as he spends weeks failing to decode notation that maps to no known system
must_land_beats: The sub-collection rooms must feel genuinely old and other — cut stone, no torches, light from the walls themselves, limestone-cold air
The manuscript must be rendered as physically strange: not parchment, slightly warm, notation that moves representationally
Gavin's weeks of failed decoding should feel like real intellectual frustration, not montage — the boredom and repetition of being stuck
pressure_source: The strangeness of the manuscript itself — it resists comprehension, its materials are alien, its notation moves in ways that are not wizarding-photograph movement but something representational and unfamiliar
state_shift: Gavin has the manuscript but cannot read it. Obsession begins as he spends weeks failing to decode notation that maps to no known system
